sourceMovie.title: My Fair Lady
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: My Fair Lady works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 10 Things I Hate About You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If My Fair Lady clicked for you, 10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and romance, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after My Fair Lady?
faq.answer: Try 10 Things I Hate About You, Enchanted, A Cinderella Story—each shares DNA with My Fair Lady while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is My Fair Lady?
faq.answer: My Fair Lady (1964) is best described as Comedy, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is My Fair Lady worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
